{{Quote|Greetings. I am Medical Android 2-1b-58, but you may call me Billingsly.|Billingsly, to a medic|<ref name="SWG:MW" />}}
'''2-1b-58''', best known as '''Billingsly''', was a [[2-1B surgical droid/Legends|2-1B surgical droid]] that worked on [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|I'm sure that Tansarii Station needs a good Medic. The droids are fine in a pinch, but nothing compared to a real doctor. There's a droid here named Billingsly in the Medical Center that coordinates the staff.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], to a medic|<ref name="SWG:YNW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|You Need Work|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
Following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Billingsly was based aboard [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]], in the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Bright Jewel system]]. During that time, he worked in the [[Medical bay/Legends|medical bay]] of Tansarii Point Station, along with [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Aja Sen]] and the [[Nurse/Legends|nurse]] [[Alisia (Gran)|Alisia]], treating injured and sick beings on the station.<ref name="SWG:MW" />

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the health situation on Tansarii Point Station had become very problematic due to voracious beasts present in the station's atrium and [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] thugs active in lower levels of the station.<ref name="SWG:Alisia">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=conversation|Alisia|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> In addition, a war erupted between the [[Car'das/Legends|Car'das]] controlling Tansarii Point Station and the [[Rak'qua]] living in the neighboring [[Station Gamma]].<ref name="SWG:LtS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Spy|Lercer the Spy|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> This led to an abnormal number of hurt people that required care and the medical staff of the station barely handled the situation.<ref name="SWG:Alisia" />

Around the same time, a medic arrived on Tansarii Point Station with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] aboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Solo recommended the medic who was looking for work to visit the medical bay of the station.<ref name="SWG:MW" /> The medic first helped Aja to treat an injured mechanic who burned his hand while repairing a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]].<ref name="SWG:HA">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Medic|Helping Aja|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> The medic was then dispatched to handle a medical emergency on the lower level of the station. An accident took place in Storage Room 01 and a technician was poisoned as he inhaled too much smoke. Billingsly was able to prepare an antitoxin and the medic successfully treated the injured technician. The droid eventually granted the medic a [[Kallidahin/Legends|Polis Massan]] bracelet and a medic uniform as a reward for the assistance provided to the medical staff of Tansarii Point Station.<ref name="SWG:ME">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Medic|Medical Emergency|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

Later, after the medic had left Tansarii Point Station, Billingsly sometimes request some assistance from the medic upon medical emergencies. When a firelight erupted between criminals in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Billingsly contacted the medic on the behalf of [[Administrator/Legends|Administrator]] [[Nillika]] of the [[Mos Eisley Medical Center]]. The medic therefore helped treat injured commoners caught in the crossfire.<ref name="SWG:CitC" /> Some times later, Billingsly received an emergency call from [[Larence Zaprin]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. His [[Zaprin family|family]] [[Zaprin Estate|living]] in the [[Gallo Mountains/Legends|Gallo Mountains]] had been attacked by voracious wild beasts and his [[Jowres Zaprin|son]] and [[Mia Zaprin|daughter]] suffered from an infection. The medic was eventually able to identify the toxin responsible from the disease and generated a cure to treat the siblings.<ref name="SWG:HC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Medic|House Call|Naboo}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Billingsly was a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:MW" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Billingsly was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]]," introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]],"<ref name="SWG:MW" /> on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref>

If a new player chose the Medic profession, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] would direct the player to talk to Billingsly after they arrived on Tansarii Point Station.<ref name="SWG:MW" /> Billingsly would first ask the player to talk to Aja Sen.<ref name="SWG:HA" /> After completing her task Billingsly would send the player to the lower levels to help a wounded man. After completing the tasks the droid would reward the player with 100 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], a Polis Massan bracelet and a Standard Issue Uniform Package (containing medic pants, a medic shirt, medic shoes and a medic vest).<ref name="SWG:ME" />
